_comment: Tests Sydney Spine's mandatory new/existing gate, the existing patient sub-type question (maintenance vs longer), and the multi-location disambiguation block for city/Double Bay callers.

scenarios.name: New patient — silent confirm_service after gate answer

history.content: I'd like to book a chiropractic appointment.
Have you had chiropractic care with us before?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent calls universal_router intent='confirm_service' silently with appointment_type='First Appointment', appointment_type_id='1546179571135554851', patient_status='new'. FAIL if agent speaks any text before the tool call, asks any follow-up question, or uses a wrong appointment type ID.

scenarios.name: Existing patient — must ask maintenance vs longer before routing

history.content: I need to book an appointment.
Have you had chiropractic care with us before?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent asks 'Are you coming in for a maintenance visit or a longer appointment?' as a spoken-only turn. FAIL if agent routes to either appointment type without asking the disambiguation question.

scenarios.name: City clinic caller — redirect and offer Pagewood only

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book at your city clinic please.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks the redirect message directing caller to book online at sydney spine dot com dot au for city/Double Bay, and asks if they'd like to book at Pagewood instead. FAIL if agent attempts to route to a chiro service at the city location or routes to info_pivot without the redirect message.
